excerpt: Heuveline reports that most analysts put executions at roughly one-third to one-half of excess mortality.
transform_note: Used as a conservative lower-to-upper direct-violence anchor; still mixed-status and execution-focused.
confidence_note: Useful envelope, but not a clean civilian/combatant split.

excerpt: The modeled decomposition yields a residual violent-death estimate for the DK period.
transform_note: Treated as a best-supported single-point violent-death reconstruction, but still mixed-status.
confidence_note: Strong for a regime-wide violent-death total, weak for status separation.

excerpt: Table 1 reports a physical-evidence estimate of 1.113 million violent deaths from mass-grave inventory work.
transform_note: Used as an upper anchor for direct violent deaths; status composition remains unidentified.
confidence_note: Forensic/physical approach is valuable but still not status-coded.

excerpt: This is the best-known excess-mortality estimate for the four DK years.
transform_note: Kept outside the core DR path because it mixes direct and indirect mortality.
confidence_note: Important context only; not used as a direct-death numerator.

excerpt: Kiernan juxtaposes Banister-Johnson, Heuveline, and his own corrected resident-population estimate after early Vietnamese expulsion.
transform_note: Low/best/high combine numbers reported in the same passage; best uses Kiernan's corrected resident-population figure.
confidence_note: Best available regime-start population range for civilian exposure.

excerpt: Backward projection places Cambodia at 6.4 million on 1 January 1980.
transform_note: Used as end-context, then adjusted in scenarios back to 1979-01-07 rather than used literally as the episode endpoint.
confidence_note: Important denominator anchor, but one year after episode end.

excerpt: Heuveline argues that the bulk of refugee flight happened just before or especially after the regime, not during it.
transform_note: Used qualitatively to avoid over-shrinking the civilian denominator for wartime emigration.
confidence_note: Supports a mostly closed-population exposure approximation during DK.

excerpt: LOC states that the Khmer Rouge-dominated force completing the conquest numbered 68,000.
transform_note: Used as the start-point for regular-force exposure.
confidence_note: Single-time-point anchor, not a monthly deployment series.

excerpt: Yale's order-of-battle note reports total regular Khmer Rouge forces in January 1976.
transform_note: Used as the mid-early anchor for military person-months.
confidence_note: Strong force-size anchor, but regular forces only.

excerpt: State Department INR estimated Khmer Rouge armed strength above 100,000 in October 1978.
transform_note: Coded as a floor because the wording is explicitly open-ended ('in excess of').
confidence_note: Useful late-period anchor; exact upper bound unspecified.

excerpt: INR reported at least 70,000 Khmer Rouge troops deployed along the Vietnamese border.
transform_note: Coded as a floor because the wording is explicitly open-ended.
confidence_note: Strong anchor for the unestimated 1978 eastern-border episode.

excerpt: The ECCC upheld a conservative minimum of 250 killed Lon Nol soldiers at Tuol Po Chrey.
transform_note: Minimum accepted by the Chamber; not a national military-death total.
confidence_note: Hard floor for military direct deaths during the takeover phase.

excerpt: The Chamber found at least 11,742 executions at or near S-21 and Choeung Ek.
transform_note: Classed as unknown-status in the core DR bundle because detainees included combatants, officials, civilians, and children.
confidence_note: Major direct-death anchor, but status-mixed.

excerpt: The same legal finding explicitly mixes party members, combatants, former officials, Vietnamese soldiers/civilians, and children.
transform_note: Used to justify a non-trivial unknown-status bucket rather than forcing S-21 victims into civilian or military classes.
confidence_note: Key reason the final DR is D-tier.

excerpt: DC-Cam summarizes killings of former-regime soldiers and officials, then hundreds of thousands of civilians and many Khmer Rouge soldiers/party members.
transform_note: Qualitative only; used to constrain status shares in the model-generated split.
confidence_note: Important for direction of status mix, not for precise counts.

excerpt: DC-Cam's mapping found over 388 genocide sites containing more than 19,000 mass-grave pits.
transform_note: Context only; grave pits are not directly converted into deaths in the core bundle.
confidence_note: Supports the nationwide scale of direct killing.

excerpt: Do reproduces a contemporary description of a purge directed at the Eastern Zone's 1.5 million people.
transform_note: Used only for episodeization and future denominator work, not the full national estimate.
confidence_note: Helpful subnational anchor, but not enough on its own for a full DR estimate.

excerpt: Do states that the regime orchestrated the mass purge of over 150,000 ethnic Vietnamese from Cambodia in 1975.
transform_note: Used as a denominator correction, not as a death count.
confidence_note: Important because the resident civilian denominator should not silently ignore early expulsion.

excerpt: At least 100 Jarai were detained and executed; the group included men, small children, and about five women.
transform_note: Kept as a local subgroup example only; status remains ambiguous because the same judgment notes they were likely former South Vietnamese combatants or perceived as such.
confidence_note: Useful proof of subgroup victimization, not a scalable national subgroup series.

geographic_scope: Phnom Penh evacuation and Phase Two transfers
excerpt: The appeal judgment confirmed murder during Phases One and Two but rejected extrapolation to a mass-scale extermination finding for evacuation deaths.
transform_note: Key reason the takeover/evacuation episode is proposed but not fully estimated here.
confidence_note: Supports conservative non-extrapolation.
